item.title: keto weight loss & mock potato soup
item.description:
cook bacon bits
consult sous chef on doneness
set aside for later
i left all the bacon grease because yolo, add two celery stalks and one yellow onion
add salt and pepper and i added butter ??, cook for ten min on medium
add whole head of cauli flower, and minced garlic (i did five cloves ?) cover in broth of your choice
After the cauliflower is soft as f immersion blend (i took out about a 1/4 cup of the broth before blended to retain thiccness)
Add cheese and heavy cream and then blend again
Garnish with the bacon, some more cheese, green onions, and some crème fraiche
item.dc:creator: shinebrightlike

item.title: The Chick-fil-A is around the corner, sir.
item.dc:creator: PatThaiCM

item.title: It's disgusting how grossly misinformed people are about this.
item.description:
Okay okay, this is a bit of a rant post, so TLDR: Reactors arent nukes, and its hard for them to go chernobyl now. We need them if we want to fix the planet. Every time I talk to people online about this people always end up going back to Chernobyl or Fukushima. The truth is it's a lot more complicated than that. Rules about safety are written in blood, thats how everything has worked since the beginning of our civilization. Just like how natural gas is made to smell like rotten eggs because in victorian england it was odorless and caused asphixiation, house fires and explosions. Modern nuclear reactors are built with many contingincies in place to stop nuclear engineers from accidentally causing a meltdown. Now lets talk about chernobyl The soviet government controlling ukraine at the time was hopelessly corrupt, that paired with the severely flawed design of the soviet reactors and human error caused by the engineers working with already outdated technology lead to the worst Nuclear accident in the history of the world. This accident wasnt unforseen however, the nuclear plant had procedures against the actions of the engineers, who ran the plant at extremely low power without following safety precautions and without proper communication. These reactors were also highly unstable at low power due to how they were designed, even so the power plant continued to be used for 14 years after the meltdown until it was shut down in the year 2000, however it remains in decommission to this day. Normally a disaster response would be immediate, but the Soviet government was more concerned with covering up the disaster instead of starting evacuation so many preventable deaths were caused as a result. To sum it up, the chernobyl disaster was caused by a perfect storm of ineptitude from all parties involved. Fukushima is a little less stupid, but was still preventable. The diesel generators that ran the pumps could have easily kept running if they had been built on higher ground. As well when the plant was built they did not pay enough attention to the risk of a tsunami, despite the plant being in a prime spot for such a disaster to occur, had they built proper protections the damage could have been greatly limited. That and the fact that the nuclear agency in charge of the plant botched the response with unnecessary beurocracy. Now lets talk about everyone's favorite talking point.
The forbidden radio cake. Actually this is what nuclear waste looks like Nuclear waste isnt some green sludge that is pumped from reactors into rivers, instead it's the spent Uranium fuel after being reenriched many times to go through the reactor again. After a few times being spent of its usefulness fissile material is packed into dirt and sand, then shipped off for storage. Where you might ask? well we simply stick it right back in the ground where it came from in the first place and seal it up. All that radiation stays in the bedrock and causes almost no pollution if disposed of properly. Remember that uranium is just a metal that we take out of the earth's crust, there's no reason we cant put it back there when we're done with it. Now a lot of people are scared living next to a nuclear power plant will cause you to get radiation poisoning, this actually couldnt be further from the truth. Major accidents are very rare, only 2 class 7 accidents have ever happened in history, so a modern power plant will be totally safe to live near and wont cause your family any problems. What you should be worried about are coal power plants. I know it sounds weird but a coal power plant actually emits more radiation into its surrounding area than a nuclear power plant ever will in its normal operating life. Nuclear power plants are also safer than all of our other forms of power generation, while fossil fuel cause tens of thousands of deaths a year, nuclear energy causes less than 1 death every year due to how safe it's become. If we're going to fix our environment, we have to use the resources we have at our disposal now, we have a clean and safe was to produce all of our energy but we're not using it, Nuclear power plants have a tiny carbon footprint and are reliable enough to completely replace fossil fuels, we just need to actually use them instead of shutting them all down. Fp edit: alright, there are many points here i missed, or worded poorly, ill be making a follow up post addressing these and expanding on the nuclear waste point
item.dc:creator: FistingWithJalapenoDoritoFingers

Sound
No sound
Sound
No sound
Sound
No sound
Sound
No sound
Sound
No sound
No sound
Sound
No sound
Sound
No sound
Sound
No sound
Sound
No sound (if there's a version with sound I want it!)
Sound
.
Sound
.
Sound
.
No sound
No sound I guess
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
It's true. I hate this. I want tobacco but I want health more just not right now

item.title: Tiny human is a quick learner
item.dc:creator: AjKaramba

item.title: This is so heartwarming
item.description:
Joshua was shaken as a baby and they were told he is unlikely to survive and will have debilitating brain damage, against all odds he is now employed!
This amazing lady and her husband look after/adopt a lot of children in need. Her posts are amazing and can be found here https://www.tiktok.com/@justthebells10 Yeah I know its Tiktoc, but she deserves all the attention she can get when it comes to her fantastic work to help kids.
item.dc:creator: GreenPinkBlueDead

item.title: I'm so happy today.
item.description:
We finally kicked these idiots out of my store. No more medical exemptions and if you refuse and try to continue doing your "shopping" we call the police. They have also said (the police) that we can use reasonable force to remove somebody they already removed for us once after he told them he was just going to come back, which of course he did, we scooped him up by the shoulders and walked him out. He got arrested (rather than driven to the end of town like they did the first time) and is being charged with mischief and trespassing. Mask up, I'm tired.
We don't care about your expemtion, we have way more shoppers who wear a mask and people like yourself should use our online shopping and not be a complete idiot and make a fool out of yourself. If you can't shop online come to the door and tell us we will set that up for you with pen and paper. We will supply a mask free of charge! Tldr: We no longer let medical exemptions into our store without a mask, we don't care about your bullshit and lies.
item.dc:creator: SemiLucidUser

item.title: 11th cakeday, random bull go!
item.description:
All stolen. Our memes.
I’m still covid free. Triple vaxed.
The gf sent these two. Gotta love her.
The Danes will know
Poor photo from a train station outside Copenhagen the other day.
I’m not ALL inappropriate humour
Oh yeah. That’s the stuff
A game for the whole family
My four year old demanded to play D&D. I was so happy.
The offspring may have covid. Waiting on a test…
So I’m trying to work with her at home.
Mild symptoms so the energy level is…bananas
It’s early, but maybe you can use this tomorrow
In lieu of cat tax as I do not have one. 11 years here. I wonder how much time I have wasted

item.dc:creator: MunkyBanana

item.title: Let there be light!
item.description:
The final touch of my first nocturne painting. This is a little night scene that I saw on an early morning commute. It was a quiet, cool morning, and I saw this lone building being lit but a light. I just had to turn around and take a picture. I really enjoyed painting this scene, and trying to create the illusion of light. I hope you all enjoy. 12x16" oil on canvas.
Edit: If you'd like to see more of my work, I post a lot on here, but I post EVERYTHING art related on my Instagram: www.instagram.com/paintings_by_adam_stevens
item.dc:creator: PilotBuilder

item.dc:creator: vertigo2712

item.title: Got my elderly mother through covid.
item.description: Retired fire medic here. Got my 97 year old mother though covid by sacrificing A LOT. Live rurally in Alaska so when my body was breaking down and I could no longer physically care for her I had to fly her to assisted living in the city. What did I learn ? Fear, uncertainty and change never end in our lives so you must always adapt. The irony is I had an abusive childhood and moved out at sixteen but she had no one else to care for her (sisters stepped up also)
item.dc:creator: TOMBinAK60
